items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Jacoby Brissett, Cardinals 'significantly' far apart in negotiations
items.desc: Jacoby Brissett and the Cardinals are reportedly "significantly" far apart in negotiations to rework the last year of his contract.
items.impact: It remains unknown if Brissett will attend next week's voluntary OTAs. This will remain a situation to monitor with Gardner Minshew and Carson Beck continuing to see more reps in his absence.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Cam Ward loses 10 pounds in offseason
items.desc: Titans QB Cam Ward says he has lost 10 pounds since last season.
items.impact: Ward weighed in at 219 pounds last season. "I think it'll help me with durability," he said. "Just being faster, getting up in the pocket, and trying to play my speed." He could potentially use his legs a little more on the ground heading into year two.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Riley Nowakowski running routes from slot in OTAs
items.desc: Riley Nowakowski was standing up and running routes from the slot in practice at OTAs.
items.impact: Nowakowski was the No. 169 overall selection by the Steelers in April. He'll figure to hold his role as a blocker, while also potentially playing a role in the slot.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Tyreek Hill (knee) 'might not be ready till midway through season'
items.desc: ESPN's Adam Schefter said Tyreek Hill "might not be ready until midway through the season."
items.impact: Hill suffered a gruesome knee injury in Week 4 of last season when he dislocated his knee, while also tearing his ACL and LCL. He has undergone multiple surgeries since his injury. He remains without a clear timetable to return as a free agent.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Malik Nabers (knee) uncertain when he'll return to field
items.desc: Giants head coach John Harbaugh said the team is not sure when Malik Nabers will return to the field.
items.impact: The Giants previously said they were hopeful Nabers would be ready for Week 1, but it appears to be uncertain as of now. Harbaugh told reporters on Thursday that Nabers' injury was "not a simple knee" injury. He remains hopeful to be on the field in training camp.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Roy Robertson-Harris suffers torn Achilles in OTAs
items.desc: Giants DT Roy Robertson-Harris suffered a torn Achilles in OTAs and is expected to miss the 2026 season.
items.impact: It is a brutal break for Robertson-Harris as he is in the final year of a two-year contract he is on with the Giants. He will undergo surgery and begin his rehab process to get healthy as a free agent next offseason.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Jaylon Johnson has not been present for voluntary OTAs
items.desc: Bears CB Jaylon Johnson has not been present for the team's voluntary organized team activities.
items.impact: These practices remain voluntary right now, but it's still a situation worth monitoring. Johnson was limited to just seven games due to injuries last season. He is expected to be healthy heading into OTAs and training camp this summer.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Jihaad Campbell (shoulder) expected to be ready for training camp
items.desc: Eagles LB Jihaad Campbell is expected to be ready for training camp.
items.impact: Campbell underwent shoulder surgery to repair a torn labrum in his left shoulder back in March of last year. He could participate in OTAs to an extent.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Brandon Aiyuk expected to be released or traded by 'summer's end'
items.desc: Cam Inman of The Mercury News' reported that 49ers WR Brandon Aiyuk "is expected to be released if not traded by summer's end."
items.impact: The news on Aiyuk as of late has been fairly quiet. It is possible he could be released by next week if he doesn't show up to the 49ers first organized team activities session on May 27th.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: R Mason Thomas signs rookie deal with Chiefs
items.desc: The Chiefs and second-round pick R Mason Thomas agreed to a four-year, fully guaranteed contract.
items.impact: Thomas was the No. 40 overall pick this year. He is slated to make an immediate impact for Kansas City.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Cooper DeJean to play safety in Eagles base defense
items.desc: The Eagles are expected to play Cooper DeJean at safety in their base defense.
items.impact: DeJean played cornerback in the team's base defense last season. Defensive coordinator Vic Fangio told reporters that the plan is for him to be a safety in that alignment this season.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Jalin Conyers released by Dolphins
items.desc: The Dolphins released TE Jalin Conyers.
items.impact: Conyers will figure to seek another opportunity in another team's offseason program.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Braeden Daniels released by Dolphins
items.desc: The Dolphins released OL Braeden Daniels.
items.impact: Daniels will figure to seek another opportunity in another team's offseason program.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Carlos Washington Jr. signs with Dolphins
items.desc: The Dolphins signed RB Carlos Washington Jr.
items.impact: Washington will compete for a depth role in the Dolphins backfield in their offensive program.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Marques Cox signs with Dolphins
items.desc: The Dolphins signed OT Marques Cox.
items.impact: Cox will compete for a depth role on the Dolphins offensive line in their offensive program.

items.author: Garrett Ball
items.title: Matthew Stafford agrees to one-year, $55 million extension
items.desc: Matthew Stafford has agreed to a one-year, $55 million extension with the Rams. He is now under contract for LA for the next two seasons.
items.impact: The Rams are in a Super Bowl window, so this is hardly a surprising move. Stafford will give them the boost they need off of an MVP season to help them continue to compete for the next two years. As for Ty Simpson, it looks like he will be on the Jordan Love trajectory where we will need to wait at least two years to really get a shot as a starter.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Keenan Allen: Return to Chargers remains possible
items.desc: Chargers GM Joe Hortiz said the door's not closed on Keenan Allen returning.
items.impact: Allen returned to the team on a one-year deal in 2025 after spending a season with the Bears. "Right now we're in a spot where we're looking at the roster and we're letting these young guys get a chance," Hortiz said. "I've had some communications with Keenan's representation and we talk. Right now, the guys that we have here, we're letting them grow and develop. Nothing but love for Keenan and that door is not closed."

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Jha'Quan Jackson works out for Texans
items.desc: The Texans hosted free agent WR Jha'Quan Jackson for a work out.
items.impact: Jackson was one of five wide receivers who worked out for Houston. He did not appear in a game with the Saints last season.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: River Cracraft works out for Texans
items.desc: The Texans hosted free agent WR River Cracraft for a work out.
items.impact: Cracraft was one of five wide receivers who worked out for Houston. He last played for the Dolphins two seasons ago.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Dee Eskridge works out for Texans
items.desc: The Texans hosted free agent WR Dee Eskridge for a work out.
items.impact: Eskridge was one of five wide receivers who worked out for Houston. He hauled in four passes in a depth role with the Dolphins last season.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Samori Toure works out for Texans
items.desc: The Texans hosted free agent WR Samori Toure for a work out.
items.impact: Toure was one of five wide receivers who worked out for Houston. He was most recently waived by the Saints.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Andrew Thomas dealing with foot and shoulder injuries
items.desc: Giants coach John Harbaugh says left tackle Andrew Thomas has foot and shoulder issues that he is working back from.
items.impact: Thomas is having his snaps managed early on. He was at practice on Thursday, but he wasn't participating in team drills.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Jack Campbell signs four-year extension with Lions
items.desc: The Lions signed LB Jack Campbell to a four-year contract extension.
items.impact: Campbell previously had his fifth-year option declined. He will be under contract with Detroit through the 2030 season.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Deion Hankins waived by Bears
items.desc: The Bears waived RB Deion Hankins.
items.impact: Hankins will figure to seek another opportunity.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Jalin Hyatt appears to tweak something during practice Thursday
items.desc: Jalin Hyatt appeared to tweak something during practice on Thursday.
items.impact: Hyatt was riding the bike on the side. Duggan notes he appeared to tweak something running routes early in practice.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Collin Johnson retires from NFL after five seasons
items.desc: Collin Johnson retired from the NFL after five seasons.
items.impact: Johnson last played for the Bears two seasons ago. He is enrolling at MIT Sloan this fall to pursue his MBA.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Isaiahh Loudermilk signs with Vikings
items.desc: The Vikings signed DT Isaiahh Loudermilk.
items.impact: Loudermilk spent the past several seasons in a depth role on the Steelers offensive line. He'll figure to step into a similar role with Minnesota.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Germie Bernard draws praise from Steelers HC Mike McCarthy
items.desc: Steelers head coach Mike McCarthy said Germie Bernard was "crushing it" whenq asked about how he's handling playing all three positions.
items.impact: McCarthy had the same thing to say about Roman Wilson. "They're crushing it, frankly....it's best for our receivers to play all three positions to create opportunities for our receivers," he said. Bernard figures to move around his rookie season, but his best chance to more playing time could be in the slot.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Matt Goncalves added 10 pounds of lean mass in offseason
items.desc: Matt Goncalves told reporters he has added 10 pounds of lean mass.
items.impact: Goncalves added he has gone from 317 up to 330 pounds. He understood he needed the strength necessary to play guard on the offensive line for the Colts.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Ashton Jeanty hopes to be on field as much as possible in 2026 season
items.desc: Ashton Jeanty told reporters he hopes to not come off the field this season.
items.impact: "If I don't have to, I don't want to come off the field," Jeanty said on his workload this season. He totaled 321 touches as a rookie with 266 carries and 55 receptions. He should only take a step forward in a new offense under Klint Kubiak.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Mason Graham played through broken rib to end last season
items.desc: Mason Graham told reporters he was playing through a broken rib at the end of last season.
items.impact: Graham totaled 49 tackles with 0.5 a sack and four pass deflections as a rookie last season. He'll look to take a step forward heading into year two.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Nolan Smith Jr. arrested for speeding and reckless driving in Georgia
items.desc: Nolan Smith Jr. was arrested Friday and accused of speeding and reckless driving in Georgia.
items.impact: According to the sheriff's office, Smith was clocked going 135 mph in a 70 mph zone. Smith made bond and was released about an hour after he was booked. He has a court date set for July 14. This will remain a situation to monitor to see if the NFL or the Eagles has a punishment for him.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Dillon Gabriel second in line in QB snaps during team drills Wednesday
items.desc: Dillon Gabriel was second in line in quarterback snaps during team drills on Wednesday.
items.impact: It is still too early to be taking what is noticed in practice too seriously. Deshaun Watson continues to lead the way in the Browns quarterback room early on. It will remain a situation to monitor as the offseason progresses.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Keon Coleman describes 2026 season as 'make or break' year
items.desc: Keon Coleman described the 2026 season as "make or break" for his future in Buffalo.
items.impact: Coleman has been unable to establish himself in the Bills wide receiver room. He has shown flashes, but he hasn't put it together. He'll remain in the WR4/WR5 range for fantasy managers heading into the season.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Harold Fannin Jr. does not practice Wednesday
items.desc: Harold Fannin Jr. did not practice at voluntary OTAs on Wednesday.
items.impact: Fannin was still with the Browns and watching from the sideline. It'll be worth monitoring to see if he returns to practice soon.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Grant Delpit not at voluntary OTAs Wednesday
items.desc: Grant Delpit was not at the Browns voluntary OTAs practice on Wednesday.
items.impact: These practices remain voluntary for now, so no need to be concerned yet. It'll be a situation to monitor heading into OTAs and training camp.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Denzel Ward not at voluntary OTAs Wednesday
items.desc: Denzel Ward was not at the Browns voluntary OTAs practice on Wednesday.
items.impact: These practices remain voluntary for now, so no need to be concerned yet. It'll be a situation to monitor heading into OTAs and training camp.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Fernando Mendoza making strong early impression with Raiders
items.desc: ESPN's Adam Schefter reports that Fernando Mendoza has already made a strong impression during his first month with the Las Vegas Raiders.
items.impact: Raiders coach Klint Kubiak noted that Mendoza has been "as advertised." Kirk Cousins got all the reps with the first-team offense at practice on Wednesday and remains the likely favorite to start Week 1 this upcoming season, but Mendoza should find his way onto the field at some point during the season.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Nate Boerkircher signs rookie deal with Jaguars
items.desc: The Jaguars signed TE Nate Boerkircher to his rookie contract.
items.impact: Boerkircher was the No. 56 overall selection by Jacksonville in April. He will compete for a depth role in the Jaguars tight end room.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Anthony Johnson Jr. signs with Bears
items.desc: The Bears signed S Anthony Johnson Jr.
items.impact: Johnson has 29 career tackles and an interception, but missed last season due to an injury. He was formerly with the Packers and Giants.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Salvon Ahmed signs with Bears
items.desc: The Bears signed RB Salvon Ahmed.
items.impact: Ahmed suffered a serious and gruesome ankle injury on a hip-drop tackle during practice last year. He has worked his way back to full health and will compete for a depth role in the Bears backfield.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Maxx Crosby (knee) expected to be ready for training camp
items.desc: Raiders head coach Klint Kubiak said Maxx Crosby is expected to be ready for training camp.
items.impact: Crosby remains with the Raiders for now after the drama that took place earlier this offseason. There haven't been any trade rumors as of late, so he'll remain with the Raiders heading into training camp.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Kirk Cousins receives all first-team snaps at OTAs Wednesday
items.desc: Kirk Cousins was getting all first-team snaps at Raiders OTAs on Wednesday.
items.impact: Fernando Mendoza was working mostly with rookies. Cousins remains the likely favorite to start in Week 1 as of now.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Sam Webb works out for Bears
items.desc: The Bears worked out veteran corner Sam Webb..
items.impact: Webb previously played for the Browns and Raiders. He is continuing to seek another opportunity.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Niko Lalos released by Patriots
items.desc: The Patriots released LS Niko Lalos.
items.impact: Lalos will figure to seek another opportunity. The Patriots signed UDFA DT Travis Shaw as the corresponding move

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Cedrick Wilson Jr. signs with Lions
items.desc: The Lions signed WR Cedrick Wilson.
items.impact: Wilson was released by the Saints. He will compete for a depth spot in the Lions wide receiver room.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Aaron Rodgers says 2026 will be his final season
items.desc: Aaron Rodgers told reporters that 2026 will be his final season.
items.impact: "Yes. This is it." Rodgers recently returned to Pittsburgh on a one-year contract. He told reporters he thought he was done after Mike Tomlin walked away, but the hiring of Mike McCarthy eventually changed his mind. He'll get one more season in before he walks away from football.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Quinshon Judkins (leg/ankle) participates in team drills Wednesday
items.desc: Quinshon Judkins participated in team drills at OTAs on Wednesday.
items.impact: Judkins is working his way back from a fractured fibula and a broken ankle he suffered in Week 16 of last season. He appears to be on track to be good to go at the start of the season as of now.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Kage Casey signs rookie deal with Broncos
items.desc: The Broncos signed fourth-round pick Kage Casey to his rookie contract.
items.impact: Casey will earn $5.58 million that includes a $1,198,208 signing bonus. He will compete for a depth role on the Broncos offensive line.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Jamil Muhammad waived by Panthers
items.desc: The Panthers waived LB Jamil Muhammad.
items.impact: Muhammad had been on a futures deal with Carolina. He'll likely another opportunity.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Heinrich Haarberg signs with Panthers
items.desc: The Panthers signed UDFA TE Heinrich Haarberg.
items.impact: Haarberg is a former starting QB at Nebraska who caught eight passes for 52 yards and a TD as a senior last season.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Montrell Johnson Jr. clears waivers, reverts to IR
items.desc: Montrell Johnson Jr. cleared waivers and reverted to to Panthers injured reserve list.
items.impact: Johnson passed through waivers unclaimed after he was waived last week.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Bryce Pierre clears waivers, reverts to IR
items.desc: Bryce Pierre cleared waivers and reverted to to Panthers injured reserve list.
items.impact: Pierre passed through waivers unclaimed after he was waived last week.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Zion Young signs rookie deal with Ravens
items.desc: The Ravens announced that second-round pick Zion Young signed his rookie deal.
items.impact: Young will compete for an immediate role as an EDGE rusher for Baltimore.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Josh Sweat not at Cardinals voluntary OTAs
items.desc: Josh Sweat was not at Cardinals voluntary OTAs on Tuesday.
items.impact: Sweat reportedly "is not particularly happy" with the Cardinals. He signed a four-year, $76.4 million contract with Arizona last offseason and totaled a career-high 12 sacks last season. It is unclear what the reason is for his absence.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Jonah Coleman signs rookie deal with Broncos
items.desc: The Broncos signed RB Jonah Coleman to a four-year contract.
items.impact: Coleman is likely to open the season as the RB3 in the Broncos backfield behind RJ Harvey and JK Dobbins, though he could hold an immediate short-yardage role. He'll remain a rookie to monitor heading into OTAs and training camp.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Lamar Jackson expected to report to Ravens OTAs soon
items.desc: Ravens coach Jesse Minter said he expects Lamar Jackson to report soon.
items.impact: Jackson was not at voluntary OTAs on Tuesday. "Lamar's been one of our leaders of the offseason program," Minter said. "Couple of things going on yesterday and today, do expect him to be back soon. We've had some great conversations, know when he's gonna be back, and I'll probably leave those between me and Lamar."

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Rashee Rice undergoes clean-up surgery on knee, to miss two months
items.desc: Rashee Rice underwent a clean-up surgery on his right knee to remove loose debris that was causing inflammation and now is expected to sideline him two months.
items.impact: Rice underwent the surgery one week before being ordered to serve 30 days in jail. He should be in line to be healthy by the end of July or early August barring any setbacks. This report comes on the same day that Rice was ordered to serve 30 days in jail after violating his probation.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Ladd McConkey does not attend voluntary OTAs practice Tuesday
items.desc: Ladd McConkey was not at the Chargers voluntary OTAs practice on Tuesday.
items.impact: Justin Herbert also did not attend. It is too early to be worried about either of them as these practices remain voluntary. The hype will be high on the Chargers offense this season with Mike McDaniel as the new offensive coordinator.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Justin Herbert misses voluntary OTAs Tuesday
items.desc: Justin Herbert did not attend Tuesday's voluntary offseason work.
items.impact: Herbert also did not attend last week's Phase 2 practice. It remains voluntary so there's no need to be worried this early in the offseason program.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Demetric Felton Jr. retires from NFL
items.desc: WR/RB Demetric Felton announced he's retiring from football.
items.impact: Felton accepted a player developmental role with the Browns. He was a sixth-round pick out of UCLA is m and is only 27 years old.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Tua Tagovailoa to split reps with Michael Penix Jr. rest of offseason
items.desc: Falcons coach Kevin Stefanski will be "very intentional" in splitting reps between Michael Penix and Tua Tagovailoa the rest of the offseason.
items.impact: Tagovailoa signed a one-year contract with the Falcons in free agency. He should see more of the first-team reps early on with Penix continuing to work his way back from knee surgery.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Michael Penix Jr. to split reps with Tua Tagovailoa in offseason
items.desc: Falcons coach Kevin Stefanski will be "very intentional" in splitting reps between Michael Penix and Tua Tagovailoa.
items.impact: Penix is continuing to work his way back from last year's injury. He was throwing in the Falcons offseason program last week. He appears on track to be fully good to go by training camp.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Taysom Hill does not have contract offer from Saints
items.desc: Saints GM Mickey Loomis said that Taysom Hill does not have a contract offer.
items.impact: Hill has been quiet so far this offseason. His future remains uncertain as of now.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Cameron Jordan has contract offer from Saints
items.desc: Saints GM Mickey Loomis said that Cam Jordan has a contract offer from the team.
items.impact: Jordan has spent 15 seasons with the Saints ever since they drafted him as a first-round pick back in 2011. His market has been quiet as of now with his future remaining uncertain.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Alex Bullock placed on reserve/retired list
items.desc: The Jaguars placed WR Alex Bullock on the reserve/retired list.
items.impact: Bullock signed with the Jags after going undrafted in April.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Tershawn Wharton (neck) undergoes surgery, likely placed on PUP list
items.desc: Panthers DL Tershawn Wharton had neck surgery and is likely to open camp on PUP list.
items.impact: Panthers head coach Dave Canales said at a Monday press conference that Wharton told the team he was "feeling a little bit of discomfort" earlier this month and that doctors felt that the condition was something that needed to be addressed surgically. Wharton does not hold a timetable to return.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Jordon Vaughn waived by Colts
items.desc: The Colts waived RB Jordon Vaughn.
items.impact: Vaughn will likely seek another opportunity.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Eli Stowers signs rookie deal with Eagles
items.desc: The Eagles signed TE Eli Stowers to his rookie contract.
items.impact: Stowers lands a four-year deal with Philadelphia. He is expected to compete for an immediate role in the Eagles tight end room.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Chris Olave, Saints have no timetable on extension
items.desc: Saints GM Mickey Loomis said the team and Chris Olave have no timetable on an extension.
items.impact: "Obviously, we'd love to have something done sooner than later, and I'm sure Chris would as well, but we're not there yet," Loomis said Monday. Olave is under contract for this season at $15.49 million under his fifth-year option.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Sean Murphy-Bunting (knee) works through drills in OTAs Monday
items.desc: Sean Murphy-Bunting was able to work through all drills at OTAs on Monday.
items.impact: Murphy-Bunting is working his way back from a torn ACL he suffered last spring. He is on track to be fully healthy at the start of the season.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Starling Thomas V (knee) works on side at OTAs Monday
items.desc: Starling Thomas V got in work with trainers on the side of the field at OTAs on Monday.
items.impact: Thomas is working his way back from a torn ACL he suffered last July. He is continuing to work his way back to full strength.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Miles Davis signs with Panthers
items.desc: The Panthers signed undrafted RB Miles Davis.
items.impact: Davis will enter OTAs competing for a depth role in the Panthers backfield.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Tywone Malone Jr. signs with Panthers
items.desc: The Panthers signed undrafted free agent DT Tywone Malone Jr.
items.impact: Malone will enter OTAs competing for a depth role on the Panthers defensive line.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Tee Higgins reports to Bengals voluntary OTAs
items.desc: Tee Higgins reported to the first day of Bengals voluntary OTAs on Monday.
items.impact: Higgins reported on Monday despite it being voluntary. He'll remain a high-upside wide receiver for fantasy managers this season.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Tamon Lynum agrees to deal with Steelers
items.desc: The Steelers signed undrafted free agent CB Tamon Lynum.
items.impact: Lynum had a tryout at the Steelers rookie minicamp. He will compete for a depth role in their secondary.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Taron Johnson sitting out of voluntary offseason workouts
items.desc: Taron Johnson is not at Raiders voluntary offseason workouts for contract reasons.
items.impact: Johnson is due $18 million the next two years that is not guaranteed. He is seeking a restructured contract with the Raiders.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Lee Hunter signs rookie deal with Panthers
items.desc: The Panthers signed second-round pick Lee Hunter to his rookie contract.
items.impact: Hunter will figure to hold an immediate role on the Panthers defensive line.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Rashad Rochelle signs with Seahawks
items.desc: The Seahawks signed WR Rashad Rochelle.
items.impact: Rochelle was at the Panthers rookie minicamp. He'll compete for a depth role in the Seahawks wide receiver room.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Brandon Cisse signs rookie deal with Packers
items.desc: The Packers signed CB Brandon Cisse to a four-year, $9.364 million contract.
items.impact: The deal includes a team option. Cisse is expected to have an immediate role as a rookie.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Max Iheanachor signs rookie deal with Steelers
items.desc: The Steelers signed OT Max Iheanachor to a four-year, $20.692 million contract.
items.impact: The deal includes a fifth-year team option as well as a $11.598 million signing bonus. Iheanachor should step into an immediate starting role.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Peter Bowden waived by Chargers
items.desc: The Chargers waived LS Peter Bowden.
items.impact: Bowden will figure to seek another opportunity heading into OTAs.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Mante Morrow signs with Chargers
items.desc: The Chargers signed WR Mante Morrow.
items.impact: Morrow will compete for a depth role in the Chargers wide receiver room.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Anterio Thompson signs rookie deal with Falcons
items.desc: The Falcons signed DL Anterio Thompson to his rookie contract.
items.impact: Thompson will compete for an immediate depth role on the Falcons defensive line.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Avieon Terrell signs rookie deal with Falcons
items.desc: The Falcons signed CB Avieon Terrell to his rookie contract.
items.impact: Terrell signed a fully-guaranteed contract with Atlanta. He figures to make an immediate impact in the Falcons secondary.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Marlin Klein signs rookie deal with Texans
items.desc: The Texans signed TE Marlin Klein to his rookie deal.
items.impact: Klein was the No. 59 overall pick by Houston this year. He'll add immediate depth in the Texans tight end room.

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.title: Emmanuel McNeil-Warren signs rookie deal with Browns
items.desc: The Browns signed second-round S Emmanuel McNeil-Warren.
items.impact: McNeil-Warren was the 58th overall pick out of Toledo. He will compete for an immediate role in the Browns secondary.
